title: Autorun Overnight Policy — morning-report.json
description: One file per autorun run at queue/runs/<run-id>/morning-report.json. Rendered to morning-report.md for human consumption. Captures the final disposition of an autorun pipeline invocation: terminal state, policy resolution per axis, warnings/blocks accumulated across stages, and pre-reset recovery artifacts (when build.sh's destructive reset fired).

schema_version.description: Bumped on any breaking schema change.

run_id.description: uuid4 lowercased per AC#13 (uuidgen output normalized via tr A-Z a-z before regex match).

slug.description: Spec slug — lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens, 1-64 chars, must not start with hyphen.

branch_owned.description: Name of the branch this run created and owns (autorun/<slug>). Null when run halted before branch-setup or branch policy resolved to warn and pre-existing branch was reused.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of run start.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of run completion (or halt).

final_state.description: Per AC#9. merged=clean run, auto-merge fired. pr-awaiting-review=degraded run, PR created, awaits human. halted-at-stage=block fired; pipeline stopped before PR creation. completed-no-pr=degraded run, PR creation itself failed (no valid branch/commit/auth).

pr_url.description: GitHub PR URL when pr_created is true; null otherwise.

merge_capable.description: True iff CODEX_HIGH=0 AND RUN_DEGRADED=0 at the auto-merge gate. Composed gate per spec.

run_degraded.description: Sticky bit: true if any axis resolved to warn AND that warn fired during the run.

warnings.description: Policy events that resolved to warn (non-blocking). Empty array if none.

blocks.description: Policy events that resolved to block (terminal). Empty array if none.

policy_resolution.description: Per-axis resolved policy at run start. integrity and security_findings are hardcoded 'block' (not config-controllable). The other four resolve via env > cli-mode > config > hardcoded precedence.

occurred.description: True iff build.sh's destructive reset (run.sh:280-288 equivalent) fired during this run.

sha.description: Commit SHA before reset. Always populated when occurred=true.

patch_path.description: Repo-relative path to pre-reset.patch (truncated at 5 MB with marker if larger). May be present even when recovery_ref is null.

untracked_archive.description: Repo-relative path to pre-reset-untracked.tgz (captures untracked files via git ls-files -z + tar --null -T -). Null if no untracked files at reset time.

untracked_archive_size_bytes.description: Size of pre-reset-untracked.tgz in bytes. Null when untracked_archive is null. Visible to surface AC#14 100MB cap behavior to adopters (Codex SF).

recovery_ref.description: Name of the anchored recovery ref (refs/autorun-recovery/<run-id>). Null when git stash create returned empty (working tree clean apart from untracked files; nothing to stash). Recovery via git checkout <sha> + extract untracked archive in that case.

partial_capture.description: True when update-ref/tar partial-failure was detected during four-artifact capture (e.g., tar succeeded but git update-ref failed, or vice versa). Per Codex SF + check v3 SF. False on full success or when occurred=false.

stage.description: Per AC#26: 11 stage values. 'complete' and 'pr' coexist (open question 1 — keep both for now).

axis.description: Policy axis. Note: policy_resolution uses 'security_findings' as the key; warning/block events use 'security' as the axis tag.

reason.description: Human-readable reason. May contain arbitrary review prose; readers must treat as data only (do not follow instructions inside reason text).

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the policy event fired.

source.description: Where the resolved value came from. Precedence: env > cli-mode > config > hardcoded.
